heading: Promoting Resilient Operations for Transformative, Efficient, and Cost-saving Transportation (PROTECT) program
label: 176 Promoting Resilient Operations for Transformative, Efficient, and Cost-saving Transportation (PROTECT) program
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section and the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Reauthorization Act of 2021, referred to in subsecs. (f)(1) and (g)(2)(B), are the date of enactment of div. A of , which was approved .Pub. L. 117–58Nov. 15, 2021
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2021 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 2021section 10003 of Pub. L. 117–58section 101 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2021 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 2021section 10003 of Pub. L. 117–58section 101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an emergency declared by the Governor of the State in which the disaster or failure occurred; or

children.label: B
children.text: an emergency or disaster declared by the President.

children.label: A
children.text: is owned, operated, or maintained by a Federal, State, Tribal, or local government;

children.label: i
children.text: to transport the public away from emergency events; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to transport emergency responders and recovery resources; and

children.label: C
children.text: is designated by the eligible entity with jurisdiction over the area in which the route is located for the purposes described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: 3 .—Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: to better anticipate, prepare for, and adapt to changing conditions and to withstand and respond to disruptions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to be better able to continue to serve the primary function of the project during and after weather events and natural disasters for the expected life of the project; or

children.label: i
children.text: reduce the magnitude and duration of impacts of current and future weather events and natural disasters to a project; or

children.label: ii
children.text: have the absorptive capacity, adaptive capacity, and recoverability to decrease project vulnerability to current and future weather events or natural disasters.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program, to be known as the “Promoting Resilient Operations for Transformative, Efficient, and Cost-saving Transportation program” or the “PROTECT program”.

children.label: A
children.text: formula funding distributed to States to carry out subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: competitive planning grants to enable communities to assess vulnerabilities to current and future weather events and natural disasters and changing conditions, including sea level rise, and plan transportation improvements and emergency response strategies to address those vulnerabilities; and

children.label: i
children.text: surface transportation assets by making the assets more resilient to current and future weather events and natural disasters, such as severe storms, flooding, drought, levee and dam failures, wildfire, rockslides, mudslides, sea level rise, extreme weather, including extreme temperature, and earthquakes;

children.label: I
children.text: serve critical local, regional, and national needs, including evacuation routes; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide access or service to hospitals and other medical or emergency service facilities, major employers, critical manufacturing centers, ports and intermodal facilities, utilities, and Federal facilities;

children.label: iii
children.text: coastal infrastructure, such as a tide gate to protect highways, that is at long-term risk to sea level rise; and

children.label: iv
children.text: natural infrastructure that protects and enhances surface transportation assets while improving ecosystem conditions, including culverts that ensure adequate flows in rivers and estuarine systems.

children.heading: Eligible Activities for Apportioned Funding.—
children.label: c Eligible Activities for Apportioned Funding.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) shall be obligated for activities eligible under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of subsection (d)(4).

children.heading: .—Planning set-aside
children.label: 2 .—Planning set-aside
children.text: Of the funds apportioned to a State under section 104(b)(8) for each fiscal year, not less than 2 percent shall be for activities described in subsection (d)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: identify the base floodplain in which the project is to be located and disclose that information to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: indicate to the Secretary whether the State plans to implement 1 or more components of the risk mitigation plan under section 322 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act () with respect to the area.42 U.S.C. 5165

children.label: i
children.text: a highway project eligible for assistance under this title;

children.label: ii
children.text: a public transportation facility or service eligible for assistance under chapter 53 of title 49; or

children.label: I
children.text: connects a port to other modes of transportation;

children.label: II
children.text: improves the efficiency of evacuations and disaster relief; or

children.label: III
children.text: aids transportation.

children.label: i
children.text: increasing marsh health and total area adjacent to a highway right-of-way to promote additional flood storage;

children.label: ii
children.text: upgrades to and installation of culverts designed to withstand 100-year flood events;

children.label: iii
children.text: upgrades to and installation of tide gates to protect highways;

children.label: iv
children.text: upgrades to and installation of flood gates to protect tunnel entrances; and

children.label: v
children.text: improving functionality and resiliency of stormwater controls, including inventory inspections, upgrades to, and preservation of best management practices to protect surface transportation infrastructure.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (e)(1), the Federal share of the cost of a project carried out using funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) shall not exceed 80 percent of the total project cost.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: ii .—Non-federal share
children.text: A State may use Federal funds other than Federal funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) to meet the non-Federal cost share requirement for a project under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, preliminary engineering and design work, and other preconstruction activities; and

children.label: II
children.text: construction, reconstruction, rehabilitation, and acquisition of real property (including land related to the project and improvements to land), environmental mitigation, construction contingencies, acquisition of equipment directly related to improving system performance, and operational improvements.

children.heading: .—Eligible planning costs
children.label: ii .—Eligible planning costs
children.text: In the case of a planning activity described in subsection (d)(3) that is carried out by a State with funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8), eligible costs may include development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, preliminary engineering and design work, other preconstruction activities, and other activities consistent with carrying out the purposes of subsection (d)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: may use not more than 40 percent of the amounts apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) for the construction of new capacity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may use not more than 10 percent of the amounts apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) for activities described in subparagraph (E)(i)(I).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In addition to funds apportioned to States under section 104(b)(8) to carry out activities under subsection (c), the Secretary shall provide grants on a competitive basis under this subsection to eligible entities described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: A State or political subdivision of a State.

children.label: B
children.text: A metropolitan planning organization.

children.label: C
children.text: A unit of local government.

children.label: D
children.text: A special purpose district or public authority with a transportation function, including a port authority.

children.label: E
children.text: An Indian tribe (as defined in section 207(m)(1)).

children.label: F
children.text: A Federal land management agency that applies jointly with a State or group of States.

children.label: G
children.text: A multi-State or multijurisdictional group of entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (F).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a State or metropolitan planning organization, developing a resilience improvement plan under subsection (e)(2);

children.label: B
children.text: resilience planning, predesign, design, or the development of data tools to simulate transportation disruption scenarios, including vulnerability assessments;

children.label: C
children.text: technical capacity building by the eligible entity to facilitate the ability of the eligible entity to assess the vulnerabilities of the surface transportation assets and community response strategies of the eligible entity under current conditions and a range of potential future conditions; or

children.label: D
children.text: evacuation planning and preparation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Using funds made available under this subsection, the Secretary shall provide resilience improvement grants to eligible entities to carry out 1 or more eligible activities under clause (ii).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: An eligible entity may use a resilience improvement grant under this subparagraph for 1 or more construction activities to improve the ability of an existing surface transportation asset to withstand 1 or more elements of a weather event or natural disaster, or to increase the resilience of surface transportation infrastructure from the impacts of changing conditions, such as sea level rise, flooding, wildfires, extreme weather events, and other natural disasters.

children.label: aa
children.text: resurfacing, restoration, rehabilitation, reconstruction, replacement, improvement, or realignment of an existing surface transportation facility eligible for assistance under this title;

children.label: bb
children.text: the incorporation of natural infrastructure;

children.label: cc
children.text: the upgrade of an existing surface transportation facility to meet or exceed a design standard adopted by the Federal Highway Administration;

children.label: dd
children.text: the installation of mitigation measures that prevent the intrusion of floodwaters into surface transportation systems;

children.label: ee
children.text: strengthening systems that remove rainwater from surface transportation facilities;

children.label: ff
children.text: upgrades to and installation of structural stormwater controls;

children.label: gg
children.text: a resilience project that addresses identified vulnerabilities described in the resilience improvement plan of the eligible entity, if applicable;

children.label: hh
children.text: relocating roadways in a base floodplain to higher ground above projected flood elevation levels, or away from slide prone areas;

children.label: ii
children.text: stabilizing slide areas or slopes;

children.label: jj
children.text: installing riprap;

children.label: kk
children.text: lengthening or raising bridges to increase waterway openings, including to respond to extreme weather;

children.label: ll
children.text: increasing the size or number of drainage structures;

children.label: mm
children.text: installing seismic retrofits on bridges;

children.label: nn
children.text: adding scour protection at bridges;

children.label: oo
children.text: adding scour, stream stability, coastal, and other hydraulic countermeasures, including spur dikes;

children.label: pp
children.text: vegetation management practices in transportation rights-of-way to improve roadway safety, prevent against invasive species, facilitate wildfire control, and provide erosion control; and

children.label: qq
children.text: any other protective features, including natural infrastructure, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: aa
children.text: the benefits of the eligible activity proposed to be carried out by the eligible entity exceed the costs of the activity; and

children.label: bb
children.text: there is a need to address the vulnerabilities of surface transportation assets of the eligible entity with a high risk of, and impacts associated with, failure due to the impacts of weather events, natural disasters, or changing conditions, such as sea level rise, wildfires, and increased flood risk; or

children.label: II
children.text: the eligible activity proposed to be carried out by the eligible entity is included in the applicable resilience improvement plan under subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Community resilience and evacuation route grants.—
children.label: B Community resilience and evacuation route grants.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Using funds made available under this subsection, the Secretary shall provide community resilience and evacuation route grants to eligible entities to carry out 1 or more eligible activities under clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: is an eligible activity under subparagraph (A)(ii), if that eligible activity will improve an evacuation route;

children.label: II
children.text: ensures the ability of the evacuation route to provide safe passage during an evacuation and reduces the risk of damage to evacuation routes as a result of future emergency events, including restoring or replacing existing evacuation routes that are in poor condition or not designed to meet the anticipated demand during an emergency event, and including steps to protect routes from mud, rock, or other debris slides;

children.label: aa
children.text: communications and intelligent transportation system equipment and infrastructure;

children.label: bb
children.text: counterflow measures; or

children.label: cc
children.text: shoulders;

children.label: IV
children.text: is for the construction of new or redundant evacuation routes, if the eligible entity notifies the Secretary that existing evacuation routes are not sufficient to adequately facilitate evacuations, including the transportation of emergency responders and recovery resources;

children.label: V
children.text: is for the acquisition of evacuation route or traffic incident management equipment or signage; or

children.label: VI
children.text: will ensure access or service to critical destinations, including hospitals and other medical or emergency service facilities, major employers, critical manufacturing centers, ports and intermodal facilities, utilities, and Federal facilities.

children.label: I
children.text: current and future vulnerabilities to an evacuation route due to future occurrence or recurrence of emergency events that are likely to occur in the geographic area in which the evacuation route is located; and

children.label: II
children.text: projected changes in development patterns, demographics, and extreme weather events based on the best available evidence and analysis.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: iv .—Consultation
children.text: In providing grants for community resilience and evacuation routes under this subparagraph, the Secretary may consult with the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, who may provide technical assistance to the Secretary and to eligible entities.

children.label: I
children.text: A State (including the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands) in, or bordering on, the Atlantic, Pacific, or Arctic Ocean, the Gulf of Mexico, Long Island Sound, or 1 or more of the Great Lakes.

children.label: II
children.text: A political subdivision of a State described in subclause (I).

children.label: III
children.text: A metropolitan planning organization in a State described in subclause (I).

children.label: IV
children.text: A unit of local government in a State described in subclause (I).

children.label: V
children.text: A special purpose district or public authority with a transportation function, including a port authority, in a State described in subclause (I).

children.label: VI
children.text: An Indian tribe in a State described in subclause (I).

children.label: VII
children.text: A Federal land management agency that applies jointly with a State or group of States described in subclause (I).

children.label: VIII
children.text: A multi-State or multijurisdictional group of entities described in subclauses (I) through (VII).

children.heading: .—Grants
children.label: ii .—Grants
children.text: Using funds made available under this subsection, the Secretary shall provide at-risk coastal infrastructure grants to eligible entities to carry out 1 or more eligible activities under clause (iii).

children.heading: .—Eligible activities
children.label: iii .—Eligible activities
children.text: An eligible entity may use an at-risk coastal infrastructure grant under this subparagraph for strengthening, stabilizing, hardening, elevating, relocating, or otherwise enhancing the resilience of highway and non-rail infrastructure, including bridges, roads, pedestrian walkways, and bicycle lanes, and associated infrastructure, such as culverts and tide gates to protect highways, that are subject to, or face increased long-term future risks of, a weather event, a natural disaster, or changing conditions, including coastal flooding, coastal erosion, wave action, storm surge, or sea level rise, in order to improve transportation and public safety and to reduce costs by avoiding larger future maintenance or rebuilding costs.

children.label: I
children.text: that addresses the risks from a current or future weather event or natural disaster, including coastal flooding, coastal erosion, wave action, storm surge, or sea level change; and

children.label: II
children.text: that reduces long-term infrastructure costs by avoiding larger future maintenance or rebuilding costs.

children.label: I
children.text: access to coastal homes, businesses, communities, and other critical infrastructure, including access by first responders and other emergency personnel; or

children.label: II
children.text: access to a designated evacuation route.

children.heading: .—Solicitations for grants
children.label: A .—Solicitations for grants
children.text: In providing grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall conduct a transparent and competitive national solicitation process to select eligible projects to receive grants under paragraph (3) and subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (4).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (3) or subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (4), an eligible entity shall submit to the Secretary an application in such form, at such time, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.label: I
children.text: as part of the application, identify the floodplain in which the project is to be located and disclose that information to the Secretary; and

children.label: II
children.text: indicate in the application whether, if selected, the eligible entity will implement 1 or more components of the risk mitigation plan under section 322 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act () with respect to the area.42 U.S.C. 5165

children.label: i
children.text: a highway project eligible for assistance under this title;

children.label: ii
children.text: a public transportation facility or service eligible for assistance under chapter 53 of title 49;

children.label: iii
children.text: a facility or service for intercity rail passenger transportation (as defined in ); orsection 24102 of title 49

children.label: I
children.text: connects a port to other modes of transportation;

children.label: II
children.text: improves the efficiency of evacuations and disaster relief; or

children.label: III
children.text: aids transportation.

children.label: i
children.text: increasing marsh health and total area adjacent to a highway right-of-way to promote additional flood storage;

children.label: ii
children.text: upgrades to and installing of culverts designed to withstand 100-year flood events;

children.label: iii
children.text: upgrades to and installation of tide gates to protect highways; and

children.label: iv
children.text: upgrades to and installation of flood gates to protect tunnel entrances.

children.heading: .—Planning grant
children.label: i .—Planning grant
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of a planning activity carried out using a planning grant under paragraph (3) shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II) and subsection (e)(1), the Federal share of the cost of a project carried out using a grant under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (4) shall not exceed 80 percent of the total project cost.

children.heading: .—Tribal projects
children.label: II .—Tribal projects
children.text: On the determination of the Secretary, the Federal share of the cost of a project carried out using a grant under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (4) by an Indian tribe (as defined in section 207(m)(1)) may be up to 100 percent.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: iii .—Non-federal share
children.text: The eligible entity may use Federal funds other than Federal funds provided under this subsection to meet the non-Federal cost share requirement for a project carried out with a grant under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, preliminary engineering and design work, and other preconstruction activities; and

children.label: II
children.text: construction, reconstruction, rehabilitation, and acquisition of real property (including land related to the project and improvements to land), environmental mitigation, construction contingencies, acquisition of equipment directly related to improving system performance, and operational improvements.

children.heading: .—Planning grants
children.label: ii .—Planning grants
children.text: Eligible project costs for activities funded with a grant under paragraph (3) may include the costs of development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, preliminary engineering and design work, other preconstruction activities, and other activities consistent with carrying out the purposes of that paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: may use not more than 40 percent of the amount of the grant for the construction of new capacity; and

children.label: II
children.text: may use not more than 10 percent of the amount of the grant for activities described in subparagraph (F)(i)(I).

children.heading: .—Limit on certain activities
children.label: ii .—Limit on certain activities
children.text: For each fiscal year, not more than 25 percent of the total amount provided under this subsection may be used for projects described in subparagraph (C)(iii).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of funds, an eligible entity may request and the Secretary may distribute funds for a grant under this subsection on a multiyear basis, as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.heading: .—Rural set-aside
children.label: ii .—Rural set-aside
children.text: Of the amounts made available to carry out this subsection for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall use not less than 25 percent for grants for projects located in areas that are outside an urbanized area with a population of over 200,000.

children.heading: .—Tribal set-aside
children.label: iii .—Tribal set-aside
children.text: Of the amounts made available to carry out this subsection for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall use not less than 2 percent for grants to Indian tribes (as defined in section 207(m)(1)).

children.heading: .—Reallocation
children.label: iv .—Reallocation
children.text: For any fiscal year, if the Secretary determines that the amount described in clause (ii) or (iii) will not be fully utilized for the grant described in that clause, the Secretary may reallocate the unutilized funds to provide grants to other eligible entities under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the Assistant Secretary of the Army for Civil Works, the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, the Secretary of the Interior, and the Secretary of Commerce; and

children.label: B
children.text: solicit technical support from the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.label: A
children.text: retain not more than a total of 5 percent of the funds made available to carry out this subsection and to review applications for grants under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: transfer portions of the funds retained under subparagraph (A) to the relevant Administrators to fund the award and oversight of grants provided under this subsection.

children.heading: Resilience Improvement Plan and Lower Non-Federal Share.—
children.label: e Resilience Improvement Plan and Lower Non-Federal Share.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State that receives funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) or an eligible entity that receives a grant under subsection (d) shall have the non-Federal share of a project carried out with the funds or grant, as applicable, reduced by an amount described in subparagraph (B) if the State or eligible entity meets the applicable requirements under that subparagraph.

children.label: aa
children.text: developed a resilience improvement plan in accordance with this subsection; and

children.label: bb
children.text: prioritized the project on that resilience improvement plan; and

children.label: aa
children.text: developed a resilience improvement plan in accordance with this subsection; and

children.label: bb
children.text: prioritized the project on that resilience improvement plan.

children.heading: .—Incorporation of resilience improvement plan in other planning
children.label: ii .—Incorporation of resilience improvement plan in other planning
I
children.text: in the case of a State or an eligible entity that is a State or a metropolitan planning organization, the resilience improvement plan developed in accordance with this subsection has been incorporated into the metropolitan transportation plan under section 134 or the long-range statewide transportation plan under section 135, as applicable; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an eligible entity not described in subclause (I), the eligible entity is located in a State or an area served by a metropolitan planning organization that incorporated a resilience improvement plan into the metropolitan transportation plan under section 134 or the long-range statewide transportation plan under section 135, as applicable.

children.heading: .—Maximum reduction
children.label: I .—Maximum reduction
children.text: A State or eligible entity may not receive a reduction under this paragraph of more than 10 percentage points for any single project carried out with funds under subsection (c) or a grant under subsection (d).

children.heading: .—No negative non-federal share
children.label: II .—No negative non-federal share
children.text: A reduction under this paragraph shall not reduce the non-Federal share of the costs of a project carried out with funds under subsection (c) or a grant under subsection (d) to an amount that is less than zero.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be for the immediate and long-range planning activities and investments of the State or metropolitan planning organization with respect to resilience of the surface transportation system within the boundaries of the State or metropolitan planning organization, as applicable;

children.label: B
children.text: shall demonstrate a systemic approach to surface transportation system resilience and be consistent with and complementary of the State and local mitigation plans required under section 322 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ();42 U.S.C. 5165

children.label: C
children.text: shall include a risk-based assessment of vulnerabilities of transportation assets and systems to current and future weather events and natural disasters, such as severe storms, flooding, drought, levee and dam failures, wildfire, rockslides, mudslides, sea level rise, extreme weather, including extreme temperatures, and earthquakes;

children.label: i
children.text: designate evacuation routes and strategies, including multimodal facilities, designated with consideration for individuals without access to personal vehicles;

children.label: I
children.text: emergency response personnel and equipment; and

children.label: II
children.text: access to emergency services, including for vulnerable or disadvantaged populations;

children.label: iii
children.text: describe the resilience improvement policies, including strategies, land-use and zoning changes, investments in natural infrastructure, or performance measures that will inform the transportation investment decisions of the State or metropolitan planning organization with the goal of increasing resilience;

children.label: I
children.text: includes a list of priority projects; and

children.label: II
children.text: describes how funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(8) or provided by a grant under the program would be invested and matched, which shall not be subject to fiscal constraint requirements; and

children.label: v
children.text: use science and data and indicate the source of data and methodologies; and

children.label: I
children.text: to respond promptly to the impacts of weather events and natural disasters; and

children.label: II
children.text: to be prepared for changing conditions, such as sea level rise and increased flood risk;

children.label: ii
children.text: describe the codes, standards, and regulatory framework, if any, adopted and enforced to ensure resilience improvements within the impacted area of proposed projects included in the resilience improvement plan;

children.label: iii
children.text: consider the benefits of combining hard surface transportation assets, and natural infrastructure, through coordinated efforts by the Federal Government and the States;

children.label: iv
children.text: assess the resilience of other community assets, including buildings and housing, emergency management assets, and energy, water, and communication infrastructure;

children.label: v
children.text: use a long-term planning period; and

children.label: vi
children.text: include such other information as the State or metropolitan planning organization considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—No new planning requirements
children.label: 3 .—No new planning requirements
children.text: Nothing in this section requires a metropolitan planning organization or a State to develop a resilience improvement plan or to include a resilience improvement plan under the metropolitan transportation plan under section 134 or the long-range statewide transportation plan under section 135, as applicable, of the metropolitan planning organization or State.

children.label: i
children.text: subject to paragraph (2), transportation and any other metrics as the Secretary determines to be necessary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: procedures for monitoring and evaluating projects based on those metrics; and

children.label: B
children.text: select a representative sample of projects to evaluate based on the metrics and procedures established under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: publish the proposed metrics in the Federal Register; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the public an opportunity for comment on the proposed metrics.

children.heading: .—Reports from eligible entities
children.label: 1 .—Reports from eligible entities
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date on which a project carried out with a grant under subsection (d) is completed, the eligible entity that carried out the project shall submit to the Secretary a report on the results of the project and the use of the funds awarded.

children.label: i
children.text: each project for which a grant was provided under subsection (d);

children.label: ii
children.text: information relating to project applications received;

children.label: iii
children.text: the manner in which the consultation requirements were implemented under subsection (d);

children.label: iv
children.text: recommendations to improve the administration of subsection (d), including whether assistance from additional or fewer agencies to carry out the program is appropriate;

children.label: v
children.text: the period required to disburse grant funds to eligible entities based on applicable Federal coordination requirements; and

children.label: vi
children.text: a list of facilities that repeatedly require repair or reconstruction due to emergency events.

children.heading: .—Final report
children.label: B .—Final report
children.text: Not later than 5 years after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Reauthorization Act of 2021, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that includes the results of the reports submitted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Treatment of Projects
children.label: h .—Treatment of Projects
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a project assisted under this section shall be treated as a project on a Federal-aid highway under this chapter.
